topic: Migrate NotificationCenter Observers to Swift Concurrency
layout_issues: Add one short pull-quote style insight using markdown blockquote.
review_issues: No concrete code examples are provided — the article describes patterns conceptually but lacks runnable snippets (e.g., a for-await-in Task that posts/handles a Notification).
No platform/version availability guidance — it doesn’t state the minimum OS versions required for NotificationCenter.notifications(named:object:) or async/await features (e.g., iOS/macOS versions).
No explicit guidance about updating UI from notification handlers — missing explicit mention to hop to the main actor / MainActor.run when handlers affect UI state.
Doesn’t discuss retain-cycle specifics (capturing self in Tasks) or show common safe capture patterns (weak self, Task cancellation checks).

Limited guidance on mixing Combine and AsyncSequence in practice — article warns about mixed paradigms but doesn’t give concrete bridging examples (e.g., converting Publisher to AsyncSequence or vice versa).
Operational/observability suggestions are high-level (structured logging, metrics) with no example log schema or recommended tooling (OSLog, MetricKit, Prometheus, etc.).
review_strengths: Clear, well-structured organization with scannable headings and a practical incremental checklist for rollout.
Accurate references to relevant APIs and concurrency primitives (NotificationCenter.notifications(named:object:), AsyncStream/AsyncThrowingStream, Task, actors, Combine).
Good decision criteria enumerated (when to prefer AsyncSequence vs Combine vs AsyncStream) that make migration choices explicit.
Actionable testing and validation guidance (XCTest async tests, profiling, memory/leaks checks) and emphasis on verifying cancellation and ordering.
Practical operational advice around logging Task lifecycle and tracking active consumer counts to detect leaks or long-lived subscribers.
